title: sambucus racemosa var. rosiflora
intro: Sambucus racemosa var. rosiflora, also known as the red-berried elder or American red-berried elder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Adoxaceae family. The plant's medicinal properties lie in its berries, leaves, and roots, which have been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Sambucus racemosa var. rosiflora include its antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of the common cold and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and syrups made from the berries, leaves, and roots, which can be used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and other infections.
